summary: Implemented three utility services: UnitConversion (kg/lbs conversion with barbell plate rounding), DataExport (JSON export of all training data with date range filtering), and DataImport (schema validation, conflict detection, and merge with skip/overwrite strategies). All services use dependency injection for testability.
keyDecisions: roundToPlate generates all achievable sums from standard plates (each 0-2 copies) rather than requiring a barbell, making it work for dumbbells and lighter weights too
DataExport and DataImport use adapter interfaces (FileSystemAdapter, ShareAdapter, FileReaderAdapter) for dependency injection, enabling testing without native expo modules
Import wraps all database writes in a transaction with rollback on error
Export converts bigint biz_key values to number for JSON serialization
Conflict detection checks biz_key existence before import, supporting both skip and overwrite strategies

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: kgToLbs(kg) and lbsToKg(lbs) with conversion factor 2.20462
displayWeight(kg, unit) formats weight with unit label
storeWeight(input, unit) converts to kg for storage (always stores as kg)
roundToPlate(kg) rounds to nearest achievable barbell plate combination
exportData(range) generates JSON file with all entity arrays filtered by date range
Export format: { training_plans, workout_sessions, ..., user_settings } arrays
getEstimatedSize(range) returns approximate file size in KB
validateFile(filePath) checks JSON schema, required fields, detects conflicts
importData(filePath, conflictStrategy) merges data with conflict resolution (skip/overwrite)
Import wrapped in transaction; rolled back on error
Unit tests for conversion accuracy, export format, import validation

notes: 56 new tests added (30 unit-conversion + 26 export/import). Full suite passes with 418 tests total. Pre-existing lint errors in other files are unrelated.
